---
title: "Synthetic Canadians: Cross-Dataset Comparison"
subtitle: "Synthetic Survey Data Quality Across Five Canadian Political Surveys"
date: today
author: "Synthetic Opinion Lab"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    fig-width: 11
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "./")
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import seaborn as sns
import json, warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

DATASETS = {
    "DC19":  {"dir": "dc19_output",  "label": "Democracy Checkup 2019",          "year": 2019, "type": "DC"},
    "DC20":  {"dir": "dc20_output",  "label": "Democracy Checkup 2020",          "year": 2020, "type": "DC"},
    "DC21":  {"dir": "dc21_output",  "label": "Democracy Checkup 2021",          "year": 2021, "type": "DC"},
    "CES19": {"dir": "ces19_output", "label": "Canadian Election Study 2019",    "year": 2019, "type": "CES"},
    "CES21": {"dir": "ces21_output", "label": "Canadian Election Study 2021",    "year": 2021, "type": "CES"},
}

# Load all comparison results
records = []
for key, meta in DATASETS.items():
    with open(f"{meta['dir']}/comparison/comparison_results.json") as f:
        res = json.load(f)
    n_real = len(pd.read_parquet(f"{meta['dir']}/clean_data.parquet"))
    for gen, gdata in res["generators"].items():
        records.append({
            "dataset": key,
            "label":   meta["label"],
            "year":    meta["year"],
            "type":    meta["type"],
            "generator": gen,
            "score":   gdata.get("overall_score"),
            "n_personas": gdata.get("n_personas"),
            "n_real":  n_real,
            "duration_s": gdata.get("duration_seconds"),
        })

df = pd.DataFrame(records)

GEN_LABELS = {"irt": "IRT", "llm_qwen": "LLM", "agent_simulation": "Agent"}
GEN_COLORS = {"IRT": "#d7191c", "LLM": "#fdae61", "Agent": "#1a9641"}
df["gen_label"] = df["generator"].map(GEN_LABELS)

# Pivot for easy display
pivot = df.pivot_table(index="dataset", columns="gen_label", values="score").reindex(
    ["DC19", "DC20", "DC21", "CES19", "CES21"]
)[["LLM", "IRT", "Agent"]]

# Add metadata
meta_df = df.groupby("dataset").first()[["label", "year", "type", "n_real"]].reindex(pivot.index)
summary = meta_df.join(pivot)
```

## Overview

This report compares synthetic data quality across **five Canadian political surveys** spanning 2019–2021. Three generators are evaluated on each dataset: a **Large Language Model** (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct via Together API), an **Item Response Theory** model (Graded Response Model), and an **Agent-based** opinion dynamics simulation.

```{python}
from IPython.display import display

display_df = summary.copy()
display_df.index.name = "Dataset"
display_df.columns = ["Survey", "Year", "Type", "N real", "LLM score", "IRT score", "Agent score"]
display_df["N real"] = display_df["N real"].apply(lambda x: f"{int(x):,}")
for col in ["LLM score", "IRT score", "Agent score"]:
    display_df[col] = display_df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.3f}")

display(display_df.style
    .set_caption("Overall quality scores by dataset and generator")
    .set_table_styles([{"selector": "th", "props": [("font-weight", "bold")]}]))
```

---

## Quality Scores by Generator

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
datasets_order = ["DC19", "DC20", "DC21", "CES19", "CES21"]
x = np.arange(len(datasets_order))
width = 0.25
offsets = [-1, 0, 1]

for i, gen in enumerate(["LLM", "IRT", "Agent"]):
    scores = [pivot.loc[d, gen] for d in datasets_order]
    bars = ax.bar(x + offsets[i]*width, scores, width, label=gen,
                  color=GEN_COLORS[gen], alpha=0.85, edgecolor='white')

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels([f"{d}\n({summary.loc[d,'label'].split()[0]} {summary.loc[d,'year']})"
                    for d in datasets_order], fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel("Overall quality score")
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax.axhline(0.5, color='grey', linestyle='--', linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.6, label='0.5 reference')
ax.set_title("Synthetic data quality scores by dataset and generator", fontweight='bold')
ax.legend()

# Shade DC vs CES regions
ax.axvspan(-0.5, 2.5, alpha=0.04, color='steelblue', label='_DC surveys')
ax.axvspan(2.5, 4.5, alpha=0.04, color='orange', label='_CES surveys')
ax.text(1.0, 0.93, "Democracy Checkup", ha='center', fontsize=9, color='steelblue', fontstyle='italic')
ax.text(3.5, 0.93, "Election Study", ha='center', fontsize=9, color='darkorange', fontstyle='italic')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## LLM Consistently Leads

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# Left: line plot showing score trends over time by generator
for gen in ["LLM", "IRT", "Agent"]:
    dc_scores = [pivot.loc[d, gen] for d in ["DC19", "DC20", "DC21"]]
    axes[0].plot([2019, 2020, 2021], dc_scores, marker='o', label=gen,
                 color=GEN_COLORS[gen], linewidth=2)
axes[0].set_title("Democracy Checkup: scores over time", fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel("Year"); axes[0].set_ylabel("Quality score")
axes[0].set_ylim(0.4, 0.9); axes[0].legend()
axes[0].set_xticks([2019, 2020, 2021])
axes[0].grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

# Right: DC vs CES comparison grouped by generator
gen_order = ["LLM", "IRT", "Agent"]
dc_means = [pivot.loc[["DC19","DC20","DC21"], g].mean() for g in gen_order]
ces_means = [pivot.loc[["CES19","CES21"], g].mean() for g in gen_order]
x2 = np.arange(3)
axes[1].bar(x2 - 0.2, dc_means, 0.35, label="DC surveys (avg)", color='steelblue', alpha=0.85)
axes[1].bar(x2 + 0.2, ces_means, 0.35, label="CES surveys (avg)", color='darkorange', alpha=0.85)
axes[1].set_xticks(x2); axes[1].set_xticklabels(gen_order)
axes[1].set_ylabel("Mean quality score"); axes[1].set_ylim(0.4, 0.9)
axes[1].set_title("Average scores: DC vs CES surveys", fontweight='bold')
axes[1].legend(); axes[1].grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print("""
**Key observations:**

- **LLM** is the top-performing generator on every dataset without exception.
- **IRT** and **Agent** are closely matched across datasets, with IRT narrowly ahead on DC surveys
  and Agent slightly ahead on CES21.
- **DC surveys score higher** than CES surveys for all generators. DC surveys use uniform
  4-point scales for most items, while CES mixes 4/5/8/9-point scales — wider scale variety
  makes distribution matching harder.
- **DC21 is the easiest dataset** (LLM = 0.803), likely due to its large sample (n = 7,576)
  and homogeneous scale structure.
""")
```

---

## Score Gap: LLM vs Best Alternative

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
datasets_order = ["DC19", "DC20", "DC21", "CES19", "CES21"]
llm_scores = [pivot.loc[d, "LLM"] for d in datasets_order]
best_alt   = [max(pivot.loc[d, "IRT"], pivot.loc[d, "Agent"]) for d in datasets_order]
gaps       = [l - b for l, b in zip(llm_scores, best_alt)]
colors     = ['#2196F3' if g > 0 else '#f44336' for g in gaps]
bars = ax.bar(datasets_order, gaps, color=colors, alpha=0.85, edgecolor='white')
ax.axhline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.8)
ax.set_ylabel("LLM score − best alternative score")
ax.set_title("LLM advantage over best non-LLM generator", fontweight='bold')
for bar, g in zip(bars, gaps):
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2, g + 0.005, f"+{g:.3f}", ha='center', fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Sample Sizes and Durations

```{python}
dur_df = df[df["generator"] == "llm_qwen"][["dataset", "n_real", "duration_s"]].set_index("dataset")
dur_df = dur_df.reindex(["DC19", "DC20", "DC21", "CES19", "CES21"])
dur_df["duration_min"] = (dur_df["duration_s"] / 60).round(1)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

# Real sample sizes
colors_type = ['steelblue' if DATASETS[d]['type']=='DC' else 'darkorange' for d in dur_df.index]
axes[0].bar(dur_df.index, dur_df["n_real"], color=colors_type, alpha=0.85, edgecolor='white')
for i, (d, row) in enumerate(dur_df.iterrows()):
    axes[0].text(i, row["n_real"] + 100, f"{int(row['n_real']):,}", ha='center', fontsize=9)
axes[0].set_ylabel("N respondents (complete cases)")
axes[0].set_title("Real survey sample sizes", fontweight='bold')
blue_p = mpatches.Patch(color='steelblue', label='Democracy Checkup')
orange_p = mpatches.Patch(color='darkorange', label='Election Study')
axes[0].legend(handles=[blue_p, orange_p])

# LLM generation time (100 personas)
axes[1].bar(dur_df.index, dur_df["duration_min"], color=colors_type, alpha=0.85, edgecolor='white')
for i, (d, row) in enumerate(dur_df.iterrows()):
    axes[1].text(i, row["duration_min"] + 0.3, f"{row['duration_min']}m", ha='center', fontsize=9)
axes[1].set_ylabel("LLM generation time (minutes, 100 personas)")
axes[1].set_title("LLM generation time by dataset", fontweight='bold')
axes[1].legend(handles=[blue_p, orange_p])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## Summary Table

```{python}
#| output: asis
rows = []
for d in ["DC19", "DC20", "DC21", "CES19", "CES21"]:
    row = summary.loc[d]
    llm_s = pivot.loc[d, "LLM"]
    irt_s = pivot.loc[d, "IRT"]
    agt_s = pivot.loc[d, "Agent"]
    n = int(row["n_real"])
    rows.append(f"| {d} | {row['label']} | {n:,} | {llm_s:.3f} | {irt_s:.3f} | {agt_s:.3f} | LLM |")

print("| Dataset | Survey | N real | LLM | IRT | Agent | Best |")
print("|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
for r in rows: print(r)
print()
print("LLM = Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Turbo via Together API (100 synthetic respondents)")
print("IRT = Graded Response Model (1,000 synthetic respondents)")
print("Agent = Opinion dynamics simulation, Watts-Strogatz network (500 synthetic respondents)")
```
